name: Prune and Orange Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and orange peel in a bowl.
2. Stir to mix well.
3. Add the Prune Butter and orange juice, and stir just until the dry ingredients are moistened.
4. Fold in the pecans if desired.
5. Coat an 8-x-4-inch loaf pan with non-stick cooking spray.
6. Spread the mixture evenly in the pan.
7. Bake at 350°F for about 40 minutes, or until a wooden toothpick inserted in the center of the loaf comes out clean.
8. Remove the bread from the oven and let sit for 10 minutes.
9. Invert the loaf onto a wire rack, turn right side up, and cool before slicing and serving.
Nutritional Facts (per slice)
Calories: 82
Fat: 0.3g
Protein: 2.4g
Cholesterol: 0mg
Sodium: 73mg
Fiber: 3g
Calcium: 15mg
Potassium: 162mg
Iron: 0.9mg.
Source: "Secrets of Fat-Free Baking" by Sandra Woodruff, RD.
Prune Butter is another recipe in this book, and is meant to be used as a substitute for all kinds of fat in baked goods. It is not available commercially without added sweetener, but 1 cup of it can be made in a food processor with 8 oz pitted prunes and 6 tablespoons water or fruit juice (I used apple juice in the batch I made for this recipe).
